title: Build Your Own Forth Interpreter
summary: Step-by-step tutorial on building a Forth interpreter. Covers stack-oriented programming, Reverse Polish Notation (RPN), REPL construction, arithmetic operations, stack manipulation (dup/drop/swap/rot/over), word definition, conditionals, loops, and running scripts. Strong educational value with hands-on approach.

title: OpenAI, the US government and Persona built an identity surveillance machine
summary: Investigative report revealing OpenAI and Persona's identity verification infrastructure. Analyzes leaked source code exposing watchlist databases, facial recognition algorithms, biometric screening against 14 categories of adverse media, and SAR filings to FinCEN. High technical depth with code-level analysis of surveillance systems.

title: Stripe reportedly makes offer to acquire PayPal
summary: Business news report about Stripe's potential acquisition of PayPal. Low technical content, primarily financial/M&A news. Not recommended for technical translation.

title: The Missing Semester of Your CS Education – Revised for 2026
summary: MIT course covering essential CS tools: command-line, development environment, version control (Git), debugging, profiling, packaging/shipping code, agentic coding with AI tools, and code quality. Updated for 2026 with AI integration. Highly valuable foundational content for programmers.

title: FreeBSD doesn't have Wi-Fi driver for my old MacBook. AI build one for me
summary: Article about using AI to develop a Wi-Fi driver for FreeBSD on old MacBook hardware. Based on title, appears to cover AI-assisted kernel/driver development. Conservative rating due to inability to access full content.
